up.units: mol per mol phytate (stoichiometric; carbon/energy source)

sec.method: predicted from carbon/redox recovery (not measured due to bicarbonate buffer)

growth_records.cite: De Vos et al. Phytate metabolism is mediated by microbial cross-feeding in the gut microbiota. Nat Microbiol (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01698-7. [PMC7618129]
growth_records.snippet: M. jalaludinii grew rapidly in phytate with a doubling time of 3.4 h... net theoretical fermentative equation as: 1 phytate + 6 H2O -> 1 3-hydroxypropionate + 0.4 lactate + 0.2 succinate + 0.2 acetate + 0.6 CO2 + 6 phosphate.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported = 3.4 h (phytate). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/3.4 = 0.2039 h-1. Product/uptake 'rates' are the reported net fermentation stoichiometric coefficients per mol phytate consumed (not per gDW/h; no biomass yield Yxs in mass units reported, so mmol/gDW/h qS/qP cannot be derived). CO2 (0.6) predicted from C/redox balance, not measured (bicarbonate buffer). Medium base (YCFA / bicarbonate-buffered medium ref 60) composition not enumerated in text; phytate 10 or 40 mM.

growth_records.cite: De Vos et al. Phytate metabolism is mediated by microbial cross-feeding in the gut microbiota. Nat Microbiol (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01698-7. [PMC7618129]
growth_records.snippet: the doubling time with myo-inositol was 7 h... converted [13C6]myo-inositol to [13C3]3-hydroxypropionate, [13C3]lactate and [13C4]succinate. The metabolite production was similar between both conditions.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported = 7 h (myo-inositol). mu = ln2/7 = 0.6931/7 = 0.0990 h-1. Same end metabolites (3-hydroxypropionate, lactate, succinate) as phytate but no explicit molar stoichiometry given for inositol, so secretion rates left unquantified.

up.units: sole carbon/energy source (dephosphorylated by M. jalaludinii)

growth_records.cite: De Vos et al. Phytate metabolism is mediated by microbial cross-feeding in the gut microbiota. Nat Microbiol (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01698-7. [PMC7618129]
growth_records.snippet: 3-hydroxypropionate was first produced and subsequently converted to propionate after 8 h in the coculture... 13C-NMR analysis confirmed 13C-labelled propionate, succinate and acetate as end metabolites from [13C6]InsP6 in the coculture.
growth_records.notes: Cross-feeding co-culture: M. jalaludinii degrades phytate to 3-hydroxypropionate which A. rhamnosivorans converts to propionate. No growth rate or specific fluxes quantified in text; captured as qualitative product profile. Cocultures reached higher cell density than M. jalaludinii monoculture (Extended Fig. 3a).
